definition: Statistically recurring patterns tied to calendar periods — certain months, weeks, or events produce predictable directional biases.
related_to: quarterly_shifts (seasonal patterns coincide with quarterly boundaries)

patterns.january_effect: Equities tend bullish early January (new year allocation)
patterns.summer_doldrums: Jul-Aug: Lower volatility, reduced institutional activity, VIX tends lower
patterns.Q4_rally: Oct-Dec: Institutional window dressing, holiday rally tendency
patterns.NFP_week: First Friday of month: increased volatility around Non-Farm Payrolls
patterns.FOMC_drift: Pre-FOMC drift upward is statistically documented in equities

how_to_use: Seasonal tendencies are CONTEXT, not signals. They increase/decrease conviction for trades already validated by layers 1-5. Never trade solely on seasonality.
